content:
Hey all - I'm trying to run the doctests on an Android 6.6 kernel and I get this error:

ERROR:root:error: unreachable `pub` item
    --> rust/doctests_kernel_generated.rs:4713:1
     |
4713 | pub fn bytes_add_one(uptr: *mut c_void, len: usize) -> Result<()> {
     | ---^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     | |
     | help: consider restricting its visibility: `pub(crate)`
     |
     = help: or consider exporting it for use by other crates
     = note: requested on the command line with `-D unreachable-pub`

error: unreachable `pub` item
    --> rust/doctests_kernel_generated.rs:4789:1
     |
4789 | pub fn get_bytes_if_valid(uptr: *mut c_void, len: usize) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
     | ---^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     | |
     | help: consider restricting its visibility: `pub(crate)`
     |
     = help: or consider exporting it for use by other crates

error: aborting due to 2 previous errors

Ultimately, I'm trying to write some unit tests using the Support KUnit tests with a user-space like syntax that was sent last year. I'm able to get that working (well, at least compiling and running the existing doctests) on rust-next with minimal changes.

Can someone give me a pointer to where doctests_kernel_generated gets generated? I'm assuming I might be able to tweak something there to get it working.

Looks like the "problem" is in the rust/kernel/uaccess.rs doctests where a couple pub fns are defined, and the compiler ultimately complains trying to compile the doctest because -D unreachable-pub is passed. Maybe there is a way not to pass these kinds of flags for doctests?

That gets past the compiler error, but I don't get any KTAP output when running the tests. (by the way - this is just on the Android 6.6 branch, without trying to patch in the user-space syntax patches referenced above)

.kunit/.kunitconfig:

CONFIG_KUNIT=y
CONFIG_RUST=y
CONFIG_RUST_KERNEL_DOCTESTS=y

My kernel .config is just make allnoconfig with this appended to the bottom (per advice from @Andreas Hindborg ) Kleis

CONFIG_64BIT=y
CONFIG_X86_64=y
CONFIG_X86=y

CONFIG_CC_OPTIMIZE_FOR_PERFORMANCE=y
CONFIG_SMP=y
CONFIG_KERNEL_XZ=y
CONFIG_SLUB=y
CONFIG_SLUB_TINY=y

CONFIG_BINFMT_ELF=y
CONFIG_BINFMT_SCRIPT=y

CONFIG_MODULES=y
CONFIG_MODULE_UNLOAD=y

CONFIG_MODVERSIONS=n
CONFIG_RUST=y

That patch series was meant for non-doctests, so it should be unrelated to the doctests (or perhaps I am confusing what you said).

As for the error, should the example be pub? i.e. you are not calling it, right? I mean, maybe we want to be able to write pub for examples, but the function isn't called, right?

As for the error, should the example be pub? i.e. you are not calling it, right? I mean, maybe we want to be able to write pub for examples, but the function isn't called, right?

Exactly - the doctest defines a pub fn as an example and then the compiler complains about that.
I made it pub(crate) in the doctest and that error went away

That patch series was meant for non-doctests, so it should be unrelated to the doctests (or perhaps I am confusing what you said).

Right, sorry to be more clear I'm starting by just seeing if I can get the doctest working on the android 6.6 branch, then I'll move onto trying to patch in the changes for non-doctests.
Is there a reason to not just write fn?
Not sure, I'm just now realizing that it looks like uaccess.rs is only in the android branch (maybe it was deleted/moved in rust-next).

Matt Gilbride said:

That gets past the compiler error, but I don't get any KTAP output when running the tests. (by the way - this is just on the Android 6.6 branch, without trying to patch in the user-space syntax patches referenced above)

.kunit/.kunitconfig:

CONFIG_KUNIT=y
CONFIG_RUST=y
CONFIG_RUST_KERNEL_DOCTESTS=y

My kernel .config is just make allnoconfig with this appended to the bottom (per advice from Andreas Hindborg ) Kleis

CONFIG_64BIT=y
CONFIG_X86_64=y
CONFIG_X86=y

CONFIG_CC_OPTIMIZE_FOR_PERFORMANCE=y
CONFIG_SMP=y
CONFIG_KERNEL_XZ=y
CONFIG_SLUB=y
CONFIG_SLUB_TINY=y

CONFIG_BINFMT_ELF=y
CONFIG_BINFMT_SCRIPT=y

CONFIG_MODULES=y
CONFIG_MODULE_UNLOAD=y

CONFIG_MODVERSIONS=n
CONFIG_RUST=y

I think you need a little more to get output from a virtual machine. Are you using the kunit python scripts to kick things off, or launching yourself?
The python script: tools/testing/kunit/kunit.py run --make_options LLVM=1 --arch=x86_64
We have not applied any uaccess.rs yet -- it is on Lore (where it is (will be?) a fn): https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20240418-alice-mm-v6-1-cb8f3e5d688f@google.com/
Oh a friend of mine sent that patch :)
ok, yea, fn will be fine, we'll change that internally

Matt Gilbride said:

Oh a friend of mine sent that patch :)

Yeah :)
The fragment of the config you have is not enabling the KUnit tests, so if you are running the kernel manually, KUnit is not configured.
Running with the run command of kunit.py should be ok though. I think that modifies the config and adds stuff required.

The fragment of the config you have is not enabling the KUnit tests, so if you are running the kernel manually, KUnit is not configured.

Running with the run command of kunit.py should be ok though. I think that modifies the config and adds stuff required.

It does - this is how I run them in my r-f-l checkout.
I don't like that, so I use the exec option instead: (cd {{path}} && tools/testing/kunit/kunit.py exec --make_options LLVM=1 --build_dir {{top}}/{{path}}-build --arch=x86_64)
I think I ran into problems at some point where the kunit script changed the config in a way that was not cool.

Oh, one other thing, I had to apply this:

modified   tools/testing/kunit/qemu_configs/x86_64.py
@@ -5,6 +5,6 @@ QEMU_ARCH = QemuArchParams(linux_arch='x86_64',
 CONFIG_SERIAL_8250=y
 CONFIG_SERIAL_8250_CONSOLE=y''',
               qemu_arch='x86_64',
-              kernel_path='arch/x86/boot/bzImage',
+              kernel_path='vmlinux',
               kernel_command_line='console=ttyS0',
               extra_qemu_params=[])

but that may be because I am not running the config generated by the python script.
Without that, there is no output from the VM through the kunit tool
ok, interesting

Dumb question - if you do something like this (which I've been doing in my rfl checkout since last we spoke):
make allnoconfig
mv .config .allnoconfig
<have another file called .rustconfig with the minimal flags above>
./scripts/kconfig/merge_config.sh .allnoconfig .rustconfig
^^ at this point if I make LLVM=1, I get a couple of prompts that I just blindly smash enter in response to.
Does the .config get updated as a result of responding to those prompts? (I am trying this in my android checkout now)
I presume so, because I don't get prompted on subsequent builds (and I am wondering if those flags need to be there for kunit to run properly as well)

Does the .config get updated as a result of responding to those prompts?

Yes, it should.
One thing to look out for when you start with fragments like this is that your fragments might conflict and that the resolution is silent and might not give you the result you want.

Looking at the thing you had to modify above...and looking at my kunit output....and looking at my filesystem...

qemu-system-x86_64 -nodefaults -m 1024 -kernel .kunit/arch/x86/boot/bzImage -append 'kunit.enable=1 console=ttyS0 kunit_shutdown=reboot' -no-reboot -nographic -serial stdio
I have no such bzImage (it's under x86_64)
oh wait ignore me, yes I do (along with a bunc hof other files in the x86 dir)

One thing to look out for when you start with fragments like this is that your fragments might conflict and that the resolution is silent and might not give you the result you want.

So doing this works to build the kernel, but the same result from kunit (and if I make the vmlinux change it just fails faster)
I can probably get some help from another colleague that works on kunit (he sent the patch series I mentioned above) next week.
